label: Content Formats
description: The physical or structural form of the work — the vessel that contains the content, independent of what that content is about.

tags.definition: Annual reference compiling statistics, facts, and forecasts organized by date or category
A curated collection of works (stories, poems, essays) by multiple authors
A work whose primary content is visual art, with minimal or supplementary text
A collection of maps, often with accompanying geographic or thematic data
A first-person account of the author's own life, written by the subject
An account of a person's life written by someone other than the subject
A work organized by dates and events, tracing a timeline
Written in journal or diary form — dated entries presented as contemporaneous
A reference work organized alphabetically, defining terms
A comprehensive reference work covering a broad range of subjects
A collection of short non-fiction prose pieces on distinct topics
A narrative told primarily through sequential art (comics format); longer form
A collection of correspondence, organized around an epistolary exchange
A specialized vocabulary or word list for a particular subject domain
Sequential art in Japanese comics format; typically serialized and read right-to-left
A first-person narrative focused on a specific period, theme, or set of experiences in the author's life
Long-form prose fiction (or narrative nonfiction); typically 40,000+ words
Mid-length prose narrative; typically 17,500–40,000 words
A work whose primary content is photographic images
A collection of cooking or craft instructions organized around preparation
A work designed for lookup rather than linear reading
Formatted as dramatic dialogue and stage directions; written for performance
A brief prose narrative; typically under 7,500 words; often collected in anthologies
A structured educational work designed for formal instruction in a subject
